_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Design, develop, and modify software systems, using scientific analysis and mathematical models to predict and measure outcome and consequences of design.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to make defensible architectural decisions under uncertainty, documenting the rationale, rejected alternatives, and conditions that would trigger re-evaluation.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-013 (AI, cosine: 0.510), AI-T-006 (AI, cosine: 0.507).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.404). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [analysis, consequences, design, develop, mathematical, measure] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, alternatives, architectural, conditions, decisions, defensible] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.857 (P=0.864, R=0.850). Balanced P/R — the 21-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (94.9%). "Design, develop, and modify software systems, using scientific analysis and math" and "Ability to make defensible architectural decisions under uncertainty, documentin" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.527 > STS 0.404 (gap +0.124). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.7%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [analysis, consequences, design, develop, mathematical, measure]. WIDAI terms: [ability, alternatives, architectural, conditions, decisions, defensible].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.124 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
